articles.title: Mark Dufner Hole In One Champion $6,740
articles.excerpt: Mark Dufner scored a big win in Oakes, ND outlasting Anthony Scarborough from Mansfield ND. Dufner, from Crookston MN took home $6,740 for 1st Place with Anthony Scarborough from Mansfield ND runner up $4,350. Congratulations Mark on an out…
articles.content: Mark Dufner scored a big win in Oakes, ND outlasting Anthony Scarborough from Mansfield ND. Dufner, from Crookston MN took home $6,740 for 1st Place with Anthony Scarborough from Mansfield ND runner up $4,350. Congratulations Mark on an outstanding tournament, and a big thank you to all the players who turned out for today's event. Full results including updated POY leaderboard. 1st Place - Mark Dufner $6,740 Runner Up - Anthony Scarborough $4,350 3rd Place - Lee Bohling $3,175 4th Place - David McDonald $2,100 5th Place - Jeremy Thury $1,700 6th Place - Paul Johnson $1,350 7th Place - Jeremy Heinze $1,050 8th Place - Thomas Brimhall $820 9th Place - Randy Kleinsasser $640 10th Place - Curt Waldie $525 11th Place - Paul Awender $525 12th Place - Brandon Johnson $525 Big shoutout to our hosts at the Angry Beaver Lodge and the Hole in One Foundation. Thank you guys! Due to the winter storm weather threat, the Buffalo City Showdown was postponed until Saturday April 15th.

articles.title: Brian Twete Wins $1,500 Poker Package in Player Appreciation Freeroll
articles.excerpt: Brian Twete from Northwood ND earned a DPT title this season by taking down the Eagle Eye tournament in Valley City back in April. He earned a seat in today's TOC and finished in 5th Place. Twete had things to do back home and announced on…
articles.content: Brian Twete from Northwood ND earned a DPT title this season by taking down the Eagle Eye tournament in Valley City back in April. He earned a seat in today's TOC and finished in 5th Place. Twete had things to do back home and announced on the very first hand of his table that he was going all in on the first hand. Go big or go home... Michelle Meza looked down at AK and called Twete's all in. But Twete ended up knocking out Michelle and doubling up his chips. So he decided at that point he might as well play it out! And that he did. Twete cruised all day with a big stack and went on to defeat Tony Uhlir headsup to capture the $1,500 Poker Package award and another trophy. Twete is excited to use the $1,500 towards an upcoming World Series of Poker event in June. Good luck Brian and congrats!

articles.title: Nick Ingolfsland Captures Speedway Title and $11,230
articles.excerpt: Nick Ingolfsland picked a great time to make his first appearance of the season, taking down a DPT-record 170 player single-day tournament field at the Speedway Event Center for $11,230. The final table featured several players in the hunt…
articles.content: When we got headsup it was Nick and Awesome Basim, with Nick flopping a full house to put Basim away. With the big win, Nick catapulted out of nowhere to challenge for Player of the Year status, but Basim's 2nd place $7,250 cash put him on top. Sunday's Bounty Tournament now becomes very interesting in terms of the point race. But no matter what happens on Sunday, players should know that the Player Appreciation Freeroll and and Tournament of Champions events will COUNT towards POY status as well. Those freeroll bonus tournaments will award points using the FPN points structure, which awards points to approximately the top 30% of the field.

articles.title: DPT Season XI *IMPORTANT UPDATES*
articles.excerpt: Attn Players! Mark this date! SUNDAY MAY 22ND DPT Season XI will be wrapping up in May in time to award three (3) $1,500 poker packages (in time for the upcoming 2022 World Series of Poker at Bally's on the strip in Las Vegas). The DPT Seas…
articles.content: Attn Players! Mark this date! SUNDAY MAY 22ND DPT Season XI will be wrapping up in May in time to award three (3) $1,500 poker packages (in time for the upcoming 2022 World Series of Poker at Bally's on the strip in Las Vegas). The DPT Season XI Tournament of Champions and Player Appreciation Freeroll events are tentatively scheduled for Sunday, May 22nd at Bar Nine in West Fargo. In a new twist, please note that players will receive BONUS points for attending these events, and winners will receive points equivalent to cash value of any prizes won. For example, the winner of the Player Appreciation Freeroll will receive $1,500 Poker Package and that win will COUNT for POINTS in the Player of the Year race. Same goes for the TOC. Starting times for these events will be announced soon. The DPT Season XI Player of the Year will be determined following those events. POY will earn a DPT POY Bracelet, and a seat in a World Series of Poker event.

articles.title: Jason Taylor Scoops a Pot
articles.excerpt: With a board of Q-A-3-J and 3 players in the hand, Jason Taylor from Dickinson led out with a bet of 2000, Clarence Schaible from Mott called on the button and the 3rd player folded. With a 9 on the river, Taylor bet 4000, and Schaible call…
articles.content: With a board of Q-A-3-J and 3 players in the hand, Jason Taylor from Dickinson led out with a bet of 2000, Clarence Schaible from Mott called on the button and the 3rd player folded. With a 9 on the river, Taylor bet 4000, and Schaible called. Taylor showed K-10 for a straight and Schaible groaned while showing A-J. After dragging that pot, Taylor sits with 40,000 chips and Scheible 20,000. Taylor won a seat to today's tournament playing poker league at Champs Paragon Bowl Wednesday night.

articles.title: Martin Short from Grand Forks is Monster Stack Champion
articles.excerpt: Martin Short and Mark Dufner had a hard fought headsup battle with Martin coming out on top, earning the Title and $7,750 cash, becoming the latest Monster Stack Champion since Cody Slusher took down the same event 2 years ago. The tourname…
articles.content: Martin Short and Mark Dufner had a hard fought headsup battle with Martin coming out on top, earning the Title and $7,750 cash, becoming the latest Monster Stack Champion since Cody Slusher took down the same event 2 years ago. The tournament features a stacked field of 122 unique players and a prize pool just shy of $30k, despite the poor weather weekend and stricter border controls in Canada due to the latest Covid spike. The tournament ran smooth, and we ended up with three (3) separate live stream segments of the Feature Table, including action from Day 1 & 2. Visit youtube.com/dakotapokertour for replays. Congrats to the 15 players who cashed in this event and we'll look forward to seeing everyone for a return at the Dead Money Open at the M&J Saloon on Saturday, February 12th (1-day format). Can win buy-in on Friday's single table satellite night, or throughout the week at the Free Poker Network League nights around Fargo.

articles.content: We're underway, at least 11 new entrants today including Paul Johnson, Thane Fliginger, Tiny, Aaron Raap, Lee Cavanaugh, Russel Johnson, Nicholas Michael, and others, to bring the unique player count to 122 and counting. Prize pool approaching $30,000. Registration is still open until 1pm. Good luck everyone! Stay tuned for Feature Table Live Stream this afternoon: www.youtube.com/dakotapokertour

articles.title: Day 2 Table Draw
articles.excerpt: Here is the table draw and chip counts for Day 2. Cards in the air at noon
articles.content: Here is the table draw and chip counts for Day 2. Cards in the air at noon

articles.title: Live Stream Coming Up
articles.excerpt: We're getting ready to come back from the dinner break. The Feature Table Live Stream is coming up, tune into YouTube - Dakota Poker Tour Channel to view. The Feature Table includes Season X POY Runner Up and former DPT Champ Brandon Dosch,…
articles.content: We're getting ready to come back from the dinner break. The Feature Table Live Stream is coming up, tune into YouTube - Dakota Poker Tour Channel to view. The Feature Table includes Season X POY Runner Up and former DPT Champ Brandon Dosch, Season X Player Appreciation Freeroll Winner Eric Dosch, and East Grand Forks veteran Mike Marek. We currently have 108 unique entries.

articles.title: Monster Stack Underway with over 100 players Registered
articles.excerpt: We've reached the 100 player mark here in Grand Forks for Day 1 of the L.i.s.t.e.n. Inc Monster Stack at Playmakers Lounge in the Canad Inn. We look forward to building a big prize pool.
articles.content: We've reached the 100 player mark here in Grand Forks for Day 1 of the L.i.s.t.e.n. Inc Monster Stack at Playmakers Lounge in the Canad Inn. We look forward to building a big prize pool.

articles.title: Brian Peck can't get enough of the action
articles.excerpt: Brian has been not killing it at the tables tonight but he is getting plenty of quality time on the felt. We won't ask how many entries he put in tonight but it's safe to say the cost of his poker tuition went up. 😂
articles.content: Brian has been not killing it at the tables tonight but he is getting plenty of quality time on the felt. We won't ask how many entries he put in tonight but it's safe to say the cost of his poker tuition went up. 😂

articles.title: Registration closed with $2320 prize pool
articles.excerpt: Registration is closed, 14 players have busted. Top 10 earn a $200 buyin for tomorrow's Monster Stack Main Event!
articles.content: Registration is closed, 14 players have busted. Top 10 earn a $200 buyin for tomorrow's Monster Stack Main Event!

articles.title: Super Satellite Underway
articles.excerpt: Cards are in the air with 32 players to start. Plenty of time to register, 15,000 starting chips. There will be single table satellites as well.
articles.content: Cards are in the air with 32 players to start. Plenty of time to register, 15,000 starting chips. There will be single table satellites as well.

articles.title: DPT Returns to Grand Forks for the Monster Stack at the Canad Inn
articles.excerpt: The DPT returns to Grand Forks and the Canad Inn this weekend for the MONSTER STACK! Did you know? You can win a FREE seat to Friday's Super Satellite by taking down a FPN poker league night this week in Grand Forks. Monday 7pm - Southgate,…
articles.content: The DPT returns to Grand Forks and the Canad Inn this weekend for the MONSTER STACK! Did you know? You can win a FREE seat to Friday's Super Satellite by taking down a FPN poker league night this week in Grand Forks. Monday 7pm - Southgate, Wednesday 7pm - EGF Legion. Saturday the main tournament kicks off at Noon - $200 buy-in gets you 60,000 in chips. This tournament has a deep stack 2-day structure with 40 minute levels. First place last time was over $7,000.

articles.title: Poker Brat Classic - Final Table Live Stream
articles.excerpt: We have our final table of 9 players set. Please note, the live stream is on a 15 minute delay. John Harms from Columbus, Nebraska remains the chip leader with 2.75 million. Live stream here-> https://youtu.be/lDvJnS8_NEY
articles.content: We have our final table of 9 players set. Please note, the live stream is on a 15 minute delay. John Harms from Columbus, Nebraska remains the chip leader with 2.75 million. Live stream here-> https://youtu.be/lDvJnS8_NEY

articles.title: Ashly Hafdahl Wins Poker Brat Classic
articles.excerpt: Congrats to Ashly Hafdahl from Fargo, ND for taking down the Poker Brat Classic and $7,640 cash! John Harms from Columbus, NE runner up, $5,000
articles.content: Congrats to Ashly Hafdahl from Fargo, ND for taking down the Poker Brat Classic and $7,640 cash! John Harms from Columbus, NE runner up, $5,000

articles.title: John Harms Chip Leader to Start Day 2
articles.excerpt: John Harms has the chip lead going into day 2 of the Poker Brat Classic at the Windbreak. The foul weather unfortunately has prevented several players from making it today. Prize pool announcement coming soon, stay tuned.
articles.content: John Harms has the chip lead going into day 2 of the Poker Brat Classic at the Windbreak. The foul weather unfortunately has prevented several players from making it today. Prize pool announcement coming soon, stay tuned.

articles.title: Player of the Year Points Update
articles.excerpt: Updated for Season XI, the Player of the Year points calculation will now account for ALL buy-ins. Previously, the points calculation included only the initial buy-in. Now, all re-buys/re-entries will be included in the total points calcula…
articles.content: Updated for Season XI, the Player of the Year points calculation will now account for ALL buy-ins. Previously, the points calculation included only the initial buy-in. Now, all re-buys/re-entries will be included in the total points calculation. The current leaderboard reflects this change.

articles.title: Early Chip Leaders
articles.excerpt: Don Drummond, Charlie Paukert, and Tyrone Cromwell are your early chip leaders, each with 225k+. Registration is open until 1pm Sunday, a full buy in gets you 90,000 chips in this deep stack event.
articles.content: Don Drummond, Charlie Paukert, and Tyrone Cromwell are your early chip leaders, each with 225k+. Registration is open until 1pm Sunday, a full buy in gets you 90,000 chips in this deep stack event.

articles.title: DPT Getting Set for Return to the Winbreak
articles.excerpt: Tournament Directory Dana Castaneda is gearing up for a good turnout at the Windbreak this weekend. This 2-day event features a super deep structure.
articles.content: Tournament Directory Dana Castaneda is gearing up for a good turnout at the Windbreak this weekend. This 2-day event features a super deep structure.

articles.title: Richard Monson Takes Down Pour Decisions
articles.excerpt: Congratulations to Richard Monson from Ray, ND for winning the Pour Decisions tournament in Tioga and $2,150 cash. Richard joins the list of DPT Season XI Tournament Champs.
articles.content: Congratulations to Richard Monson from Ray, ND for winning the Pour Decisions tournament in Tioga and $2,150 cash. Richard joins the list of DPT Season XI Tournament Champs.

articles.title: Chips in the Air in Tioga
articles.excerpt: We're underway at Pour Decisions in Tioga with 3 tables. With deep stacks, there is lots of play in this tournament and registration does not close until after the dinner break around 5pm.
articles.content: We're underway at Pour Decisions in Tioga with 3 tables. With deep stacks, there is lots of play in this tournament and registration does not close until after the dinner break around 5pm.

articles.title: Tommy Hill Takes Down Season XI Opener
articles.excerpt: Tommy Hill outlasted 38 players to win the Season XI Opening Tournament to open up the early lead for DPT Season XI Player of the Year!
articles.content: Tommy Hill outlasted 38 players to win the Season XI Opening Tournament to open up the early lead for DPT Season XI Player of the Year!
